content.full_text:
Frrn Porrrrclr PnacrrcBs C orrurssroN
P.O. Box 807 .,128 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April6, 2001
Barbara Heller, Councilmember
City of San Rafael
11 ShannonLane
San Rafael, CA 94901
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-029
Dear Ms. Heller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTION
Are you prohibited from accepting travel certificates which you received upon
receipt of your credit card from First USA Bank, Mileage plus Visa?
CONCLUSION
Because the travel certificates. are given by First USA Bank, Mileage Plus Visa in
the regular course of business to members of the public without regard to oflicial status,
these certificates do not constitute gifts under the Act, and you are not prohibited from
accepting them.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-029
Page No. 2
FACTS
You recently applied for a First USA Bank, Mileage Plus Visa. When you were
accepted for this credit card and upon receipt of your Visa card, you were given travel
certificates as a welcome to the United Mileage Plus Platinum card program.
ANALYSIS
Under section 89503, elected officers of a local government agency are
prohibited from accepting gifts totaling more than $320 from any single source in a
calendar year. (Sections 89503(a) and (Q; Regulation 18940.2(a).) Elected oflicers are
also required to report on an annual statement of economic interests gifts aggregating $50
or more in value from a single source in a calendar year. (Section 87200 et seq.; Section
87207(a)(l) ) As a council member for the City of San Rafael, you are subject to these
provisions.
Section 82028 of the Act defines "gift" as follows
"(a) 'Gift' means, except as provided in subdivision (b),
any payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration ofequal or greater
value is not received and includes a rebate or discount in
the price of anything of value unless the rebate or discount
is made in the regular course of business to members of the
public without regard to official status. ..."
We have previously advised that an official who receives a free ticket or pass
under an airline's frequent flyer program, available to all members of the public, has not
received a gift and is not required to disclose the free airline ticket or pass on his or her
statement of economic interests . (Francis Advice Letteq No. A-00-231; Ackerman
Advice Letter, No. A-97-044; Odom Advice Letter, No. I-91-280.) In those cases, the
airlines offered their frequent flyer program of free tickets and annual passes in the
regular course of business to all members of the public.
Similarly here, First USA Bank, Mileage Plus Visa has a policy of offering travel
certificates to customers who sign uf for their United Mileage Plus Platinum card
program. Because the credit card company offers these travel certificates in the regular
course of business to members of the public without regard to official status, the travel
certificates you received from the airline do not constitute a gift under section 8ZO2B of
the Political Reform Act. As a result, your travel certificates are not subject to the Act's
gift limit, and you do not need to report them on your statement of economic interests.2
2 Our advice is limited to whether the travel voucher constitutes a reportable gift under the
Political Reform Act. As noted in the.4 ckerman Advice Letter, supra, we do not advise whether ofEcials
may keep free tickets or travel vouchers eamed while on official travel, as this may be the subject of
agency personnel mles. You may wish to check with your agency's persornel officer, city attorney or
county counsel concerning these rules.


File No. 4.-01-029
Page No. 3
In your letter, you specifically ask about Article )([I, Section 7, of the California
Constitution, which states:
"[a] transportation company may not grant free passes or
discounts to anyone holding an offrce in this state; and the
acceptance of a pass or discount by a public officer, other
than a Public Utilities Commissioner, shall work a
forfeiture of that office."
Because the Commission intelprets and enforces only the Act, you should contact the
Attorney General's office if you wish additional information about this constitutional
provision.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Bo
StaffCounsel,
Division

accepting them.

receipt of your credit card from First USA Bank, Mileage plus Visa?

CONCLUSION: Because the travel certificates. are given by First USA Bank, Mileage Plus Visa in
